vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-804: Epic D: Archaeological audit & pre-1.0 cleanup

plan.title: Epic D: Archaeological audit & pre-1.0 cleanup
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: Repo carries accumulated debris (234 stashes vs 106 at audit time, ~7 stale ahead-of-remote branches, local main behind origin, 9 flagged 'orphaned' artifacts) that must be reconciled before 1.0 ships. The 2026-04-23 audit (docs/prds/active/1.0-audit-results.md) already disposed the 9 flagged items: 5 of 6 unique items are FULLY INTEGRATED, stash@{18} is recommended DROP + 2 follow-up issues. What remains is ACTING on those dispositions plus the broader cleanup ACs in the issue body.
narratives.Proposal: Sequential cleanup in three waves: (1) branch triage — re-verify current state of the 7 named branches, then push-or-abandon each independently; (2) main reconcile — fast-forward local main to origin/main via update-ref; (3) stash cleanup — re-classify the 234 current stashes with a conservative pattern filter, human-review the drop list (work.inspect gate), then drop drop-safe stashes, then handle stash@{18} per audit recommendation. Finalize with policy verification, git gc safety check (dry-run only), and an updated audit-results doc committed to main.

autoDecisions.summary: stash@{18} disposition = DROP + file 2 follow-up GitHub issues (event-backed review status; unknown-phase diagnostic UI).
autoDecisions.rationale: Per docs/prds/active/1.0-audit-results.md §6: the stash regresses 8 HEAD features (inline/embedded props, pinned-session validation, planningState query, ready-to-start sort, 5 shared component extractions, deliberately-disabled DnD). The only 2 real wins are small standalone tickets. The audit explicitly recommends DROP + follow-ups.

autoDecisions.summary: Stash drop classifier = strict allowlist of label prefixes (pre-merge:, pre-spawn:, pre-ship:, pre-sync:, pre-release:, pre-rebase:, wip-auto:, spawn-stash:, retry-debris:, release-scratch:, temp-stash-*, rebase-temp-*, swarm-overlap-rebase-temp, lockfile-temp, merge-verify-fix-stash, *-pipeline-state-files-*).
autoDecisions.rationale: Conservative — false positives (dropping a salvageable stash) are recoverable only via 90-day reflog grace; false negatives (preserving a drop-safe stash) are cheap. The classifier emits an explicit DROP / PRESERVE / UNKNOWN list, and work.inspect must validate the DROP list before the drop bead runs.

autoDecisions.summary: review-temp:PAN-NNN stash filter = drop only when (a) PR for PAN-NNN is merged/closed AND (b) no active workspace at workspaces/feature-pan-NNN/ exists.
autoDecisions.rationale: Active review cycles still need their temp stashes. The 188 review-temp stashes dominate the list; safe culling of completed-cycle ones is the highest-impact mechanical drop.

autoDecisions.summary: Default branch triage decision = abandon (delete local ref) unless the unique-to-local commits represent un-shipped work AND the underlying issue is still open.
autoDecisions.rationale: Named branches (pan-428, pan-446, pan-711, pan-714, pan-698, pan-598-integration) have been stale 4+ weeks and are now 1k+ commits diverged from main as main moved forward. The 'ahead by 1' from the original audit means 1 unique commit, almost certainly already absorbed or superseded. Bias toward delete; reflog preserves recoverability for 90 days.

autoDecisions.summary: pan-598-integration (now feature/pan-598 locally) = DELETE if any remaining unique commits are reachable from a merged PR or have equivalent content already on main; otherwise rebase the genuinely-unique commits to a fresh branch named integration/pan-598-leftovers and push.
autoDecisions.rationale: Original audit: '12 of 85 commits don't match main messages'. By now those 12 are either integrated (we've seen other PAN-598-adjacent disposition above) or weren't deemed important enough to land. Default delete; salvage only verified-unique work.

autoDecisions.summary: CLAUDE.md stash-hygiene policy bead = verify-only, not rewrite.
autoDecisions.rationale: CLAUDE.md already documents naming categories (pre-merge:/pre-spawn:/review-temp:/salvageable:), drop-on-completion rules, 4-week triage cadence, and 90-day reflog recovery — added 2026-04-23 as part of this Epic D work. Avoid drift; the existing policy already covers all required elements.

autoDecisions.summary: git gc safety check = dry-run via `git fsck --unreachable --no-reflogs` + `git reflog expire --dry-run --expire=now --all` + `git gc --dry-run`. Do NOT run actual `git gc`.
autoDecisions.rationale: The CLAUDE.md policy explicitly relies on the 90-day reflog grace for recovery. Actual gc would shorten that window. Dry-run gives us the data we need (what would be lost) without destroying it.

autoDecisions.summary: Final audit-results doc updates committed in-place at docs/prds/active/1.0-audit-results.md.
autoDecisions.rationale: PRD docs are checked-in. Updating in place keeps history reviewable in git log. Post-1.0, a separate cleanup can move 'active' PRDs to 'completed/'.

autoDecisions.summary: Main-branch reconcile uses `git update-ref refs/heads/main origin/main` (no checkout).
autoDecisions.rationale: main is checked out in the parent repo at /home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/. Attempting `git checkout main` from the worktree would fail. update-ref is safe — it moves the ref without touching any working copy.

items.title: Re-verify current state of all 7 named ahead-of-remote branches

narrative.Action: Check each of the 7 originally-flagged branches against current refs. For each: does the local branch still exist? What's the current ahead/behind vs origin/main? What unique commits (`git log --oneline origin/main..<branch>`) remain? Output an updated table in 1.0-audit-results.md mapping original-audit-state → 2026-05-25 state → recommended action (push / delete / salvage).

subItems.title: For each of feature/pan-428, feature/pan-446, feature/pan-711, feature/pan-714 (or fix/pan-714-specialist-session-recovery), feature/pan-698, pan-598-integration (or feature/pan-598): record exists/missing, ahead/behind counts vs origin/main, and the list of unique-to-local commits.
Updated branch-state table is appended to docs/prds/active/1.0-audit-results.md under a new section '## 2026-05-25 Branch state re-verification'.
For each branch the table names a recommended action (push / delete / salvage) consistent with the autoDecision defaults (abandon unless verified-unique work + open issue).

items.title: Triage feature/pan-428 (push or delete per branch-state report)

narrative.Action: Execute the verify-branch-state recommendation for feature/pan-428. Default abandon: `git branch -D feature/pan-428` + delete any backup/pan-428-* siblings reachable from current main. If push: `git push origin feature/pan-428` and link the resulting PR/state in audit-results.

subItems.title: feature/pan-428 local ref either deleted or pushed to origin per branch-state report.
Any backup/pan-428-* branches reachable from current main are also deleted (or explicitly preserved with reason recorded in audit-results).
Action and final state for feature/pan-428 recorded in audit-results table.

items.title: Triage feature/pan-446 (push or delete per branch-state report)

narrative.Action: Execute the verify-branch-state recommendation for feature/pan-446. Default abandon: `git branch -D feature/pan-446` + delete any backup/pan-446-* siblings. The 1.0-stabilization-plan also flagged: 'closing feature/pan-446 left an orphan worktree at .claude/worktrees/pan-446-merge + temp branch temp/pan-446-merge' — clean both if still present.

subItems.title: feature/pan-446 local ref either deleted or pushed to origin per branch-state report.
.claude/worktrees/pan-446-merge and temp/pan-446-merge — if present — are removed (worktree pruned, temp branch deleted).
Action and final state for feature/pan-446 recorded in audit-results table.

items.title: Triage feature/pan-711 (push or delete per branch-state report)

narrative.Action: Execute the verify-branch-state recommendation for feature/pan-711 (was 8 ahead in original audit — the largest of the simple branches). Default abandon if all unique commits are already on main in equivalent form; push if genuinely unique unshipped work.

subItems.title: feature/pan-711 local ref either deleted or pushed to origin per branch-state report.
Any backup/pan-711-* or fix/pan-711-* branches reachable from current main are also deleted.
Action and final state for feature/pan-711 recorded in audit-results table.

narrative.Action: Resolve PAN-714 branch lineage. fix/pan-714-specialist-session-recovery exists locally — confirm whether this is a successor/rename of feature/pan-714 or a separate branch. Execute push-or-delete per branch-state report for whichever ref still exists locally.

subItems.title: fix/pan-714-specialist-session-recovery (and feature/pan-714 if it exists) either deleted or pushed per branch-state report.
The relationship between fix/pan-714-* and feature/pan-714 (if any) documented in audit-results.

items.title: Confirm feature/pan-698 is fully cleaned (already absent locally per current state)

narrative.Action: feature/pan-698 is not in current `git branch` output — already cleaned. Confirm via reflog/branch listing; also verify the salvaged refactor/issue-status-events branch (commit 812cc2cf per PRD) still exists locally OR on origin (per PAN-808 follow-up). Record final state in audit-results.

subItems.title: feature/pan-698 confirmed absent from local branch list and from origin (or status documented if it reappeared).
refactor/issue-status-events branch confirmed present (local or origin) with commit 812cc2cf — salvaged edits not lost.
Any remaining .claude/worktrees/pan-698-* artifacts or temp branches removed.

narrative.Action: feature/pan-598 exists locally (renamed/successor to pan-598-integration). Original audit: 85 ahead, 26 behind; 12 of 85 commits don't match main commit messages. Re-check the 12 — if all 12 are now equivalent to commits on main (by patch-id or content), delete the branch. If any unique work remains, cherry-pick those commits onto a fresh integration/pan-598-leftovers branch, push, link from audit-results, then delete feature/pan-598.

subItems.title: Each of the unique-to-feature/pan-598 commits compared against current main; verdict 'integrated' or 'unique' recorded for each.
If any commits are genuinely unique: integration/pan-598-leftovers branch created with those commits, pushed to origin, link recorded in audit-results.
feature/pan-598 local ref deleted after disposition recorded.

narrative.Action: Local main is 0 ahead / 1 behind origin/main (confirmed 2026-05-25). Run `git fetch origin main` then `git update-ref refs/heads/main origin/main` to fast-forward local main without touching any worktree. Do NOT `git checkout main` (it's checked out in the parent repo).

subItems.title: `git rev-list --left-right --count origin/main...main` reports 0 0 after the operation.
Parent-repo working tree at /home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/ is unaffected (no checkout occurred from this worktree).

items.title: Re-classify the 234 current stashes into DROP / PRESERVE / UNKNOWN lists

narrative.Action: Stash count has grown from 106 (audit time) to 234. Re-classify by label prefix using the conservative allowlist from autoDecisions: DROP if label starts with one of the enumerated safe prefixes (pre-merge:, pre-spawn:, pre-ship:, pre-sync:, pre-release:, pre-rebase:, wip-auto:, spawn-stash:, retry-debris:, release-scratch:, temp-stash-*, rebase-temp-*, swarm-overlap-rebase-temp, lockfile-temp, merge-verify-fix-stash). For review-temp:PAN-NNN: DROP only when PAN-NNN's PR is merged/closed AND no active feature/pan-NNN workspace exists. PRESERVE all salvageable:* and any label not matching a known pattern (mark UNKNOWN). Emit the three lists as a section in docs/prds/active/1.0-audit-results.md so the inspect gate can review before any drops happen.

subItems.title: Three explicit lists (DROP / PRESERVE / UNKNOWN) appended to docs/prds/active/1.0-audit-results.md with each stash's index, label, and on-branch.
Every salvageable:* stash appears in PRESERVE.
For each review-temp:PAN-NNN stash in DROP, the doc cites evidence that PAN-NNN's PR is merged/closed AND no active workspace at workspaces/feature-pan-NNN/ exists.
Any stash whose label doesn't match an enumerated safe prefix is in UNKNOWN (and therefore NOT dropped), not in DROP.
Classification table totals reconcile: DROP + PRESERVE + UNKNOWN equals the live `git stash list | wc -l` at the time the bead runs.

items.title: Drop the classify-stashes DROP list (newest-index-first batch)

narrative.Action: Resolve each DROP-list stash to its commit SHA via `git rev-parse stash@{N}`, then drop newest-index-first (so earlier indices remain valid throughout the batch). After the batch, record the new total stash count in audit-results and verify the remaining list matches PRESERVE + UNKNOWN.

subItems.title: Every stash in the classify-stashes DROP list is removed from `git stash list`.
Post-drop `git stash list | wc -l` equals (PRESERVE + UNKNOWN) from the classification.
Each dropped stash's commit SHA is logged in audit-results so reflog recovery within 90 days is possible if needed.

items.title: File 2 follow-up issues for stash@{18}, then drop the stash

narrative.Action: Per docs/prds/active/1.0-audit-results.md §6 recommendation: (1) Use `gh issue create` to file 'Inspector: use event-backed review status instead of 15s polling' (references DetailPanelLayout.tsx, selectReviewStatus, KanbanBoard.tsx pattern). (2) Use `gh issue create` to file 'Inspector: unknown-phase diagnostic UI' (references InspectorPanel, showUnknownPhaseDiagnostic). (3) Verify both issue URLs exist and record them in audit-results. (4) Resolve stash@{18}'s commit SHA and `git stash drop`. Strict order: never drop before both issues are filed and recorded.

subItems.title: GitHub issue 'Inspector: use event-backed review status instead of 15s polling' filed with body referencing DetailPanelLayout polled fallback, useDashboardStore(selectReviewStatus(...)) pattern already in KanbanBoard.tsx, and refetchInterval: eventReviewStatus ? false : 15000 target.
GitHub issue 'Inspector: unknown-phase diagnostic UI' filed with body referencing InspectorPanel, showUnknownPhaseDiagnostic, containers count + lifecycle reason when phase is unknown.
Both issue URLs added to docs/prds/active/1.0-audit-results.md §6 BEFORE the stash is dropped.
stash@{18}'s commit SHA logged in audit-results, then the stash dropped.

items.title: Verify CLAUDE.md stash-hygiene policy is complete (no rewrite)

narrative.Action: CLAUDE.md already documents stash-hygiene policy (added 2026-04-23). Verify it covers: (a) naming categories with prefixes (pre-merge:, pre-spawn:, review-temp:, salvageable:), (b) drop-on-completion rules per category, (c) 4-week triage cadence for non-salvageable stashes, (d) 90-day reflog recovery window. If any of these elements are missing or have drifted, add them with a single, surgical edit. If all four elements are present and correct, this bead is a no-op verify — record the verification in audit-results and close.

subItems.title: CLAUDE.md '## Stash Hygiene' section contains all four required elements (naming categories, drop-on-completion rules, triage cadence, reflog recovery).

If verification passes with no changes: audit-results notes 'CLAUDE.md stash-hygiene policy verified, no changes required'. If edits were needed: the diff is minimal and targeted to the missing elements only.

items.title: Verify git gc would not lose anything valuable (dry-run only)

narrative.Action: Run `git fsck --unreachable --no-reflogs`, `git reflog expire --dry-run --expire=now --all`, and `git gc --dry-run` from the workspace. Compare unreachable-commit output against the PRESERVE + UNKNOWN stash lists and against any salvaged commits we deliberately want to keep (e.g. 812cc2cf for PAN-808). Confirm none of those would be reaped. Do NOT run actual `git gc` — the 90-day reflog grace per CLAUDE.md is the user's recovery window.

subItems.title: Output of `git fsck --unreachable --no-reflogs` captured and appended to audit-results.
Cross-check confirms no PRESERVE or UNKNOWN stash commits, and no salvaged commits (812cc2cf and others noted in dispositions), appear in the gc dry-run reap list.
No actual `git gc` was executed — only dry-run / fsck / expire-dry-run.

narrative.Action: Update audit-results doc with: (1) header 'Status: Epic D complete', (2) re-verification of original 6 unique dispositions still valid, (3) the new 2026-05-25 branch state table and final branch dispositions, (4) the stash classification + drop log, (5) stash@{18} follow-up issue URLs and drop confirmation, (6) git gc dry-run output and safety verdict, (7) updated stash count (post-cleanup). Commit the doc update. Do NOT close PAN-804 issue here — that's the pipeline's job after merge.

subItems.title: docs/prds/active/1.0-audit-results.md has 'Status: Epic D complete' at the top and contains all seven listed sections.
Doc lists final post-cleanup stash count and post-cleanup branch count.
Doc update is committed to the feature/pan-804 branch with a commit message referencing PAN-804.
All seven issue-body acceptance criteria from PAN-804 are mapped to specific sections of the audit-results doc (traceability).
